title: Supreme Leader

result_summary.note: Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.
Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.

final_assessment:
## Military Assessment - Supreme Leader of Persia

**Current Force Disposition:**
Our military stands at 15 active units positioned entirely within the Mashriq theater. Ground forces (8 units) form our backbone, with key positions at Kermanshah facing Iraq, Tehran central defenses protecting our nuclear sites, and southern formations at Bushehr and Bandar Abbas controlling the Strait of Hormuz. Our tactical air force (6 units) is dispersed across central Isfahan, Shiraz, and eastern positions, with hardened reserves. A single S-300/S-400 air defense unit protects our nuclear facilities in central Iran.

**Strategic Context:**
We are actively at war with both Columbia (USA) and Levantia (Israel), facing a two-front confrontation. Our hybrid regime maintains moderate stability (4/10) despite severe inflation (50%) and limited treasury reserves. We possess strategic missile capabilities but remain at nuclear level 0.

**Force Balance Analysis:**
Columbia has deployed massive forces against us - 34 observable units including 13 tactical air, 11 naval, and strategic missiles. Their forces surround us: tactical air in Poland, Germany, Gulf states, and UAE; naval forces throughout the Arabian Sea, Red Sea, and Mediterranean. Levantia concentrates all 9 units at (6,10), directly threatening our western approaches with F-35I, F-15I fighters and layered air defenses.

**Key Vulnerabilities:**
We face overwhelming air superiority disadvantage (6 vs 16+ enemy tactical air units) and complete naval inferiority against Columbia's carrier groups and amphibious forces. Our concentrated positioning limits strategic depth, while enemy forces enjoy global mobility and multiple approach vectors into our homeland.

content_blocks.text: I'll assess Persia's military posture by gathering the necessary intelligence. Let me start by examining our forces and strategic situation.
